description: Documented malicious or compromised RubyGems packages. RubyGems supply-chain incidents 2019-2024 cover both maintainer-account compromise (rest-client, strong_password, bootstrap-sass) and namespace squatting / dependency confusion patterns.

entries.name: rest-client
entries.description: Maintainer account compromise. Backdoored versions exfiltrated environment data to a pastebin URL and could eval remote code via a hidden Pastebin fetch on every load. The gem had ~113M downloads at the time.

entries.name: strong_password
entries.description: Maintainer account compromise. v0.0.7 fetched a Pastebin URL and eval'd its content on load, providing a remote-code-execution backdoor for any Rails app that loaded the gem.

entries.name: bootstrap-sass
entries.description: Maintainer credentials compromised; a backdoored 3.2.0.3 release contained a hidden hook that executed arbitrary code at assets:precompile time, giving CI-time RCE for any Rails build that pulled the gem.

entries.name: noice
entries.description: Typosquat of `voice` (and other short-name gems) that exfiltrated environment variables on require. Removed within days of disclosure.

entries.name: atlas_client
entries.description: Dependency confusion against a MongoDB-internal `atlas_client` gem. Published as a public RubyGem to capture internal Gemfile resolution. Part of the Alex Birsan dependency-confusion disclosure wave.

entries.name: rua
entries.description: Typosquat of `rua` (Rust user agent utility) with a credential-harvesting payload that ran on require. Targeted developers porting Rust-side tooling to Ruby builds.

entries.name: google-protobuf
entries.description: Namespace-confusion artifact between `google-protobuf` (Google's official gem) and `protobuf` (community gem) led to attacker-uploaded pre-releases briefly resolving over the legitimate gem in mixed Gemfile.lock states.

entries.name: paypal-permissions
entries.description: Suspicious gem masquerading as a PayPal SDK helper; on require, exfiltrated environment variables to a remote endpoint. Removed within 24h of disclosure.

entries.name: railes
entries.description: Typosquat of `rails` with an info-stealer payload. Particularly dangerous because `bundle install` errors at the legitimate `rails` name would push developers to copy a wrong name into Gemfile.
